rationale: All observed activity originates from a machine account (user-01$) accessing IPC$/lsarpc and SYSVOL shares on host-01.corp.local, which is consistent with standard Windows domain Group Policy refresh behavior. The SYSVOL accesses in EV-001 through EV-003 target the two well-known default GPO GUIDs ({31B2F340...} and {6AC1786C...}) reading gpt.ini files — a textbook GP client enumeration pattern. The repeated lsarpc accesses via IPC$ (A0, EV-004 through EV-007) reflect normal LSARPC/NETLOGON RPC calls made during domain authentication and policy processing. No anomalous share targets, lateral movement indicators, or unusual access masks are present.
